selfcheck.chosen_text: Asignación sobre slices que pueden ser view/copy (chained assignment)

Theory to_numeric errors=; hints errors='coerce' on S15-T3 exercises.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Filas, columnas y provenance/hash del artefacto

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "score": [0.9, 0.4]})
df = df.set_index("cliente_id")
print(df.index.tolist())
exercises.note: Hints set_index + index.tolist(); values from theory Series example.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.Series([0.9, 0.4], index=["C001", "C002"], name="score")
print(s["C002"])
exercises.note: Theory code block shows exact Series pattern.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s1 = pd.Series([0.9, 0.4], index=["C001", "C002"])
s2 = pd.Series([0.1, 0.2], index=["C002", "C003"])
print(s1.add(s2, fill_value=0).sort_index())
exercises.note: Hints s1.add(s2, fill_value=0) + sort_index; ids from packet C00x.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
raw = "a,b\n1,2\n3,NA\n"
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(raw), na_values=["NA"])
print(int(df["b"].isna().sum()))
exercises.note: Hints read_csv StringIO na_values + isna().sum().

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
raw = "fecha,x\n2024-03-01,1\n2024-03-02,2\n"
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(raw), parse_dates=["fecha"])
print(str(df["fecha"].dtype))
exercises.note: Hints parse_dates; demo shows datetime64[ns].

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
raw = "cliente_id,monto,z\nC001,1,9\nC002,2,8\n"
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(raw), usecols=["cliente_id", "monto"])
print(df.columns.tolist())
exercises.note: Hints usecols; verify z not present.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "score": [0.4, 0.9]})
print(df.loc[df.score >= 0.5, "cliente_id"].tolist())
exercises.note: Starter has df; hints loc score>=0.5.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [1.0, 2.0]})
print(df.assign(doble=df["score"] * 2)["doble"].tolist())
exercises.note: Hints assign(doble=...); print double list.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame([[1, 2], [3, 4]])
print(df.iloc[1, 0])
exercises.note: Hints iloc[1,0] → 3.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [0.2, 0.9]})
df.loc[df.score < 0.5, "flag"] = "x"
print(df["flag"].fillna("").tolist())
exercises.note: Hints loc mask flag='x'; fillna('').

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [0.2, 0.9, 0.7]})
subset = df.loc[df.score > 0.5].copy()
subset["ok"] = True
print(subset["ok"].tolist())
exercises.note: Hints subset copy + ok=True.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"score": [0.2, 0.9]})
c = df.copy()
c.iloc[0, 0] = 99
print(df["score"].tolist())
exercises.note: Hints mutate copy; print original.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["lima", "Lima"]})
df["region"] = df["region"].str.title().astype("category")
print(df["region"].dtype.name)
exercises.note: Hints title then category; print dtype name.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.to_numeric(["1", "a", "3"], errors="coerce")
print(s.tolist())
exercises.note: Hints to_numeric errors=coerce; tolist with nan.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-01", "no-fecha"], errors="coerce")
print(int(s.isna().sum()))
exercises.note: Hints isna().sum(); NaT counts as na.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"monto": ["1", "x"]})
before = int(df["monto"].isna().sum())
coerced = pd.to_numeric(df["monto"], errors="coerce")
after = int(coerced.isna().sum())
print(after - before)
exercises.note: Hints compare isna before/after; fixture 1 new NaN.
Hints if col not in columns; print missing.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"]})
df["cliente_id"] = df["cliente_id"].astype("string")
print(str(df["cliente_id"].dtype))
exercises.note: Hints astype('string'); print str(dtype).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
buf = StringIO()
df.to_csv(buf, index=False)
buf.seek(0)
rt = pd.read_csv(buf)
print(rt.columns.tolist())
exercises.note: Hints to_csv index=False + read_csv; columns.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
from io import BytesIO
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1]})
xbuf = BytesIO()
df.to_excel(xbuf, index=False, engine="openpyxl")
print(len(xbuf.getvalue()) > 0)
exercises.note: Hints engine openpyxl; getvalue() > 0.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
contract = {c: str(df[c].dtype) for c in df.columns}
print(dict(sorted(contract.items())))
exercises.note: Hints dict comprehension; sorted keys; demo contract pattern.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2, 3]})
print(int(df.memory_usage(deep=True).sum()) > 0)
exercises.note: Hints memory_usage(deep=True).sum(); print int > 0.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0]})
manifest = {"rows": int(len(df)), "columns": df.columns.tolist()}
print(manifest["rows"])
exercises.note: Hints len(df) + columns.tolist(); print rows.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd, hashlib
df = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
blob = df.to_csv(index=False).encode()
print(hashlib.sha1(blob).hexdigest()[:8])
exercises.note: Hints sha1 hexdigest[:8]; encode utf-8; final demo similar.
